lines.english: Lesson 29
lines.chinese: 第29课
lines.raw: Lesson 29 | 第29课

lines.english: The hovercraft
lines.chinese: 气垫船
lines.raw: The hovercraft | 气垫船

lines.english: What is a hovercraft riding on when it is in motion?
lines.chinese: 气垫船开动时，它骑在什么上面？
lines.raw: What is a hovercraft riding on when it is in motion? | 气垫船开动时，它骑在什么上面？

lines.english: Many strange new means of transport have been developed in our century,
lines.chinese: 本世纪人们发明了许多稀奇古怪的交通工具，
lines.raw: Many strange new means of transport have been developed in our century, | 本世纪人们发明了许多稀奇古怪的交通工具，

lines.english: the strangest of them being perhaps the hovercraft.
lines.chinese: 其中最奇特的也许要算是气垫船了。
lines.raw: the strangest of them being perhaps the hovercraft. | 其中最奇特的也许要算是气垫船了。

lines.english: In 1953, a former electronics engineer in his fifties, Christopher Cockerell,
lines.chinese: 1953年，一位50多岁、曾任电子工程师的克里斯托弗·科克雷尔，
lines.raw: In 1953, a former electronics engineer in his fifties, Christopher Cockerell, | 1953年，一位50多岁、曾任电子工程师的克里斯托弗·科克雷尔，

lines.english: who had turned to boat-building on the Norfolk Broads,
lines.chinese: 他已转行在诺福克湖区制造船只，
lines.raw: who had turned to boat-building on the Norfolk Broads, | 他已转行在诺福克湖区制造船只，

lines.english: suggested an idea on which he had been working for many years to the British Government and industrial circles.
lines.chinese: 他把多年来一直致力于研究的一种设想，呈报给了英国政府和工业界。
lines.raw: suggested an idea on which he had been working for many years to the British Government and industrial circles. | 他把多年来一直致力于研究的一种设想，呈报给了英国政府和工业界。

lines.english: It was the idea of supporting a craft on a 'pad', or cushion, of low-pressure air, ringed with a curtain of higher pressure air.
lines.chinese: 他的设想是：让航行器由一个低压气垫支撑，气垫四周由一道高压空气幕环绕。
lines.raw: It was the idea of supporting a craft on a 'pad', or cushion, of low-pressure air, ringed with a curtain of higher pressure air. | 他的设想是：让航行器由一个低压气垫支撑，气垫四周由一道高压空气幕环绕。

lines.english: Ever since, people have had difficulty in deciding whether the craft should be ranged among ships, planes,
lines.chinese: 从那时起，人们就很难确定这种航行器究竟是船、是飞机，
lines.raw: Ever since, people have had difficulty in deciding whether the craft should be ranged among ships, planes, | 从那时起，人们就很难确定这种航行器究竟是船、是飞机，

lines.english: or land vehicles--for it is something in between a boat and an aircraft.
lines.chinese: 还是陆上交通工具——因为它介于船和飞机之间。
lines.raw: or land vehicles--for it is something in between a boat and an aircraft. | 还是陆上交通工具——因为它介于船和飞机之间。

lines.english: As a shipbuilder, Cockerell was trying to find a solution to the problem of the wave resistance which wastes a good deal of a surface ship's power and limits its speed.
lines.chinese: 作为一名造船工程师，科克雷尔当时正试图解决波浪阻力问题，波浪阻力消耗了水面船只的大部分动力，并限制了它的航速。
lines.raw: As a shipbuilder, Cockerell was trying to find a solution to the problem of the wave resistance which wastes a good deal of a surface ship's power and limits its speed. | 作为一名造船工程师，科克雷尔当时正试图解决波浪阻力问题，波浪阻力消耗了水面船只的大部分动力，并限制了它的航速。

lines.english: His answer was to lift the vessel out of the water by making it ride on a cushion of air, no more than one or two feet thick.
lines.chinese: 他的答案是让船只骑在厚度不超过一两英尺的气垫上，从而使其脱离水面。
lines.raw: His answer was to lift the vessel out of the water by making it ride on a cushion of air, no more than one or two feet thick. | 他的答案是让船只骑在厚度不超过一两英尺的气垫上，从而使其脱离水面。

lines.english: This is done by a great number of ring-shaped air jets on the bottom of the craft.
lines.chinese: 这是通过航行器底部大量的环形喷气口完成的。
lines.raw: This is done by a great number of ring-shaped air jets on the bottom of the craft. | 这是通过航行器底部大量的环形喷气口完成的。

lines.english: It 'flies', therefore, but it cannot fly higher--its action depends on the surface, water or ground, over which it rides.
lines.chinese: 因此它“飞行”，但不能飞得更高——它的运行依赖于它所行驶的水面或地面。
lines.raw: It 'flies', therefore, but it cannot fly higher--its action depends on the surface, water or ground, over which it rides. | 因此它“飞行”，但不能飞得更高——它的运行依赖于它所行驶的水面或地面。

lines.english: The first tests on the Solent in 1959 caused a sensation.
lines.chinese: 1959年在索伦特海峡进行的首次试验引起了轰动。
lines.raw: The first tests on the Solent in 1959 caused a sensation. | 1959年在索伦特海峡进行的首次试验引起了轰动。

lines.english: The hovercraft travelled first over the water, then mounted the beach, climbed up the dunes, and sat down on a road.
lines.chinese: 气垫船先是在水面上行驶，然后登上沙滩，爬上沙丘，最后停在一条公路上。
lines.raw: The hovercraft travelled first over the water, then mounted the beach, climbed up the dunes, and sat down on a road. | 气垫船先是在水面上行驶，然后登上沙滩，爬上沙丘，最后停在一条公路上。

lines.english: Later it crossed the Channel, riding smoothly over the waves, which presented no problem.
lines.chinese: 后来它穿越了英吉利海峡，平稳地在海浪上行驶，这没有造成任何问题。
lines.raw: Later it crossed the Channel, riding smoothly over the waves, which presented no problem. | 后来它穿越了英吉利海峡，平稳地在海浪上行驶，这没有造成任何问题。

lines.english: Since that time, various types of hovercraft have appeared and taken up regular service.
lines.chinese: 从那时起，各种型号的气垫船相继问世，并投入了定期运营。
lines.raw: Since that time, various types of hovercraft have appeared and taken up regular service. | 从那时起，各种型号的气垫船相继问世，并投入了定期运营。

lines.english: The hovercraft is particularly useful in large areas with poor communications such as Africa or Australia;
lines.chinese: 气垫船在非洲或澳大利亚等交通不便的广大地区尤其有用；
lines.raw: The hovercraft is particularly useful in large areas with poor communications such as Africa or Australia; | 气垫船在非洲或澳大利亚等交通不便的广大地区尤其有用；

lines.english: it can become a 'flying fruit-bowl', carrying bananas from the plantations to the ports;
lines.chinese: 它可以变成一个“飞行水果盘”，将香蕉从种植园运到港口；
lines.raw: it can become a 'flying fruit-bowl', carrying bananas from the plantations to the ports; | 它可以变成一个“飞行水果盘”，将香蕉从种植园运到港口；

lines.english: giant hovercraft liners could span the Atlantic;
lines.chinese: 巨型气垫船班轮可以横跨大西洋；
lines.raw: giant hovercraft liners could span the Atlantic; | 巨型气垫船班轮可以横跨大西洋；

lines.english: and the railway of the future may well be the 'hovertrain',
lines.chinese: 而未来的铁路很可能就是“气垫列车”，
lines.raw: and the railway of the future may well be the 'hovertrain', | 而未来的铁路很可能就是“气垫列车”，

lines.english: riding on its air cushion over a single rail, which it never touches, at speeds, up to 300 m.p.h.--the possibilities appear unlimited.
lines.chinese: 它们在单轨上方的气垫上行驶，永不接触轨道，时速可达300英里——其可能性似乎无限。
lines.raw: riding on its air cushion over a single rail, which it never touches, at speeds, up to 300 m.p.h.--the possibilities appear unlimited. | 它们在单轨上方的气垫上行驶，永不接触轨道，时速可达300英里——其可能性似乎无限。
